title: Lecture 12: What is Convoy Effect | FCFS CPU Scheduling Algorithm

segments.text: Hello ji , how are you all? this is lakshay
Today we have reached OS lecture no-12, we will start discussing about process scheduling algorithm , which is important part of process management
Before that you should know some terms , which we will be using in coming lectures
Lets see what is process scheduling?
we have already discussed about this in previous lectures , but lets discuss it again quickly
we have a ready queue , CPU, in CPU's run state all processes comes , and exits from here
Here STS dispatches any process , here will lots of processes like P1 ,P2, P3...
LTS provides the processes at ready queue
OS picks process from the ready queue , and gives to CPU, depending upon the algorithm that you are using
Process are executed , after some time quantum process comes back to ready queue, and goes to waiting state
In this way things works
So your work to pick from ready queue , which process will be picked , choosing it
Then scheduling it is done by CPU scheduler , it tells the process scheduling algorithm
Selection process is done by scheduling algorithm
I also call it CPU scheduling algorithm , giving control of any process to CPU is done by dispatcher
So what is the work of CPU scheduler? whenever CPU is idle , then have to pick one process from ready queue
It will be given to CPU, and it will be going on, we call it CPU scheduling
Before moving ahead lets give shout out to our sponsors

So to clear these interviews , your OS part must be strong

We have two type of scheduling algorithms , 1.non-preemtive scheduling
Ones you given process to CPU, you have P1 process
This is your CPU, ones you given it to CPU
It will occupy CPU until it is terminated
or goes to wait state , i/o
These are the two ways in non preemptive scheduling algorithm , it will leave CPU when it will be terminated
Or wait/ i/o
I call it non preemptive scheduling method, ones it got the CPU, it will not leave it
What we are missing here ? time quantum is missing here ,we studied time sharing
While executing if time quantum has finished , CPU will leave that process , and process will go to ready queue
Timing sharing is not there in non preemptive
2. preemptive scheduling
It happens that when it will be terminated it will leave the CPU
When it will go to wait state, then also it will leave CPU
But there is one more thing , when time quantum is expired
Then also process free CPU, goes to ready queue , so these are the two ways of CPU scheduling
If I want to compare them , what is starvation ? any process is not getting the CPU
If any job has occupied CPU for longer hours
Other jobs in ready queue are not getting the time , so they are starving
If I compare , in which process starvation will be more ? Non preemptive will have the more process starvation
Because time sharing part is missing in non preemptive , if I got a job which is CPU intensive
And there is no i/o instruction , it will terminate after 20 -30 minutes .Rest processes will go to starvation
So that's why there is more starvation in non preemptive method
In preemptive there is no starvation because of time sharing, it is less , every process will get the chance because time quantum will expire
Other process will get the chance
There is less starvation in preemptive
Which has the more CPU utilization?
You may have got the idea by seeing the starvation
Preemptive has the more CPU utilization, because more processes get the chance
So CPU will work on more no. of processes at a time
In CPU there is high CPU utilization, 3rd
Overhead
In preemptive there is more overhead, why?
Because aside that i/o, termination, there is extra overhead that , if time quantum is 1 sec , then process will be changed after every 1 sec
CPU will have to execute new process after 1 sec, STS will give new process to it, so there is more overhead
Basically if we are changing in 1se , then 10 processes will be changed in 10 sec , so there is more overheads , this is
Comparison of preemptive and non preemptive scheduler
Lets discuss goals , goals of CPU scheduling algorithms
If I am using my OS, or designing it
When I would be coding the management part , the process scheduling algorithm that I will use
On which parameters I will make it, what will be full filled by that algorithm
Lets discuss about that goals , 1.Maximum CPU utilization, we are studying about it from the staring
CPU is a important resource , I don't want it to wait
2.Minimum turn around time . What is TAT?
Whenever job 1st comes to ready queue to the termination time
P1 came to ready queue , it deed i/o , gone to wait state
again came to ready queue
When it will exit, the in between time , is the turn around time
I want it to be less , if process has came to ready queue , I want it to be executed as soon as possible
3. Minimum wait time , what do you mean by it?
I want process to wait for CPU for very less time
Whenever any process goes to ready queue , CPU preempts it
Now it wants to execute another process , in that case I want least waiting time
If there is more waiting then there will be more starvation, 4.Minimum response time
What is response time ? it is the time when process came to ready queue , and it got CPU for 1st time
The difference , your process came to ready queue , when it got the CPU for 1st time , I am calling response time
I want when any process comes to ready queue , I want it to get CPU as soon as possible
At least its program should start to execute
It should not happen that process has entered the ready queue, but it doesn't got CPU for single time
I also not want this , 5.
Max.throughput, what is throughput?
It is the no. of processes completed per unit time , this is the measure to measure any CPU scheduling algorithm
I want my through put to be the maximum so that it can take maximum no. of process
Why we are doing this ? The main goal is the maximum CPU utilization, I want my system to be fast
So I am deciding these goals , I am keeping them in mind
Lets elaborate these points , through notes
So we have written all the definitions , throughput - no. of processes completed per unit time
Then there is arrival time , time when process is arrived at the ready queue, you have a system
A time line is going on
In that clock at which point the process came to ready queue will be called arrival time
Burst time -Time required by process for execution
Actually the time required to process to be executed
Assume there is no other process , there is only one process
How much time it will required , we are calling it burst time
Turnaround time - time taken from the 1st time process enters ready state till it terminates
Now I will discuss completion time , time taken till process gets terminated
After arrival it got response time , then it got scheduled , then went to wait, again came to ready queue
again scheduled , time quantum expired again went to ready queue , in this way your process is travelling in different states
Completion time is , when long term scheduler actually put it into ready queue , till it executed
The difference is the completion time., you can get confused what is turnaround time
it is CT-AT, completion time - arrival time , I am calling it turnaround time , then wait time
Time process spends waiting for CPU ,WT=TAT-BT, turnaround time -burst time
Why it is WT=TAT-BT, when that process came to ready queue ,till it will be terminated
BT is the actual time that it will take if it is the only process in the system
I want it to be less , WT=TAT-BT
, so we have discussed , lets move to our 1st scheduling algorithm
FCFS- 1st come 1st serve , it is the simplest algorithm
The process which will come 1st in ready queue , I will send it to CPU,
P1 ,P2 ,P3 , all have came in line like this
Now what will FCFS scheduler will do , it will dispatch the one which has came 1st
When P1 will be executed , then P2 ,then P3, it is the simplest
But there is a big problem in this algorithm , Convoy effect, before understanding it
Lets say we have an example , there are some processes , like
Like this , I have created a scenario
There is a table of processes , where PID is written
We have given the arrival time , and the burst time
We want to find all the values , so we will use a technic Gantt chart
Now how will I create it , when I say t=0
Which process came at t=0
The one which has the 1st arrival time , in this case it is 0, I will schedule it 1st, P1
Up to when it will run , its burst time is 20, it will run up to 20 units
After that P2 will come in the memory, its burst time is 2
Gantt chart tells the time , if 20 sec has been completed , 20+2=22sec
Then P3 will come , 22+2=24sec
If I say , what is the completion time ? P1 's actual completion time is 20 sec
P2 ->22 sec , its 24 sec
If I try to find out the turn around time , it is CT-AT
Lets find it, it will be 20 , 21 ,23
WT=TAT-BT ,here it will be 0, because it got executed when it came
It waited 19 sec , this 20 sec , there is mistake it will be 22
22-BT , will be 20,
If I do the sum, if I see this , these are the destructive waiting time
This si my ready queue ,, this is waiting time , now if I find the average , it will be 13
My average waiting time is 13
If I change the order of processes , if i take P1 process to last
1st came P2 , then P3 ,then P1
P2 ,P3 burst time is less , P1 has the most burst time , lets see the average waiting time
1st P2 , then P3 ,2+2=4 units , P1 , 24
If I write the completion time , this taken 2 ,then 4 ,24
If I find the turnaround time , it is CT-AT, it will be 2 sec , its 3 sec
Its 22 sec , if I try to find WT, it will be 0 ,1 ,2
If now I find the average waiting time , it will be 1 unit
This called convoy effect, the job having the maximum burst time
If it comes first , then rest jobs will have to wait
Because if the 1st job will run 20 sec , rest jobs will have to wait for very long time
Average waiting time of this system is 13, we increased because of 1st job, if I place it at 3rd position
Then my average waiting time is 1 sec
We call it convoy effect, If I read its definition
If there is any process that have higher BT, it will have major impact on average BT of rest processes , we show it
If 1st will have the more BT, then waiting time for rest will increase
It is not necessary that convoy effect happens in processes or in scheduling algorithm only
Not only in CPU , if you talk about another resource , and one process is taking more time , and rest
are not able to use it, so there waiting time will increase , I call it convoy effect
I can call it poor management of resources , because resource is for all, there should be no partiality
This is my goal , for CPU scheduling
So we discussed some jargons , what is AT ,BT ,TAT ,CT, response time
We also understood the goals , what is preemptive and non preemptive scheduling
What is convoy effect, it is asked directly in interviews
So this is a interview question, lets end this video
We will meet in some next lecture , Good Bye!!
